# Marathon Training Framework

## Default Philosophy

Marathon training for Carrera Run emphasizes:
- **Consistency over intensity** — steady buildup with managed progression
- **Safety and longevity** — avoiding injury through conservative load increases
- **Evidence-based periodization** — proven training structures from sports science

## Default Configuration for Marathon Plans

### Training Volume
- **Target weekly running load:** 50+ km during stable build weeks
- **Peak weeks:** 55-70 km depending on athlete experience
- **Down weeks:** 30-40% reduction every 3-4 weeks (recovery block)

### Weekly Structure (4 Runs Per Week)
- **Monday:** Easy or Rest
- **Tuesday:** Quality (Tempo, Threshold, or VO₂ Max)
- **Wednesday:** Easy
- **Thursday:** Quality or Easy (depending on plan phase)
- **Friday:** Rest / Light Cross-training
- **Saturday:** Long Run (primary weekly focus)
- **Sunday:** Rest / Easy

### Long Run Day
- **Default:** Saturday
- **Adjacent rest days:** Friday & Sunday (minimum)
- **Progression:** Conservative week-to-week increases (typically +2-3 km/week)

## Periodization Phases

### Base Phase (Weeks 1-4)
- Focus: Build aerobic base and consistency
- Weekly structure: 2 quality + 2 easy runs
- Long run: Moderate progression (up to 18-20 km)
- Intent: Establish routine and prepare for build phase

### Build Phase (Weeks 5-12)
- Focus: Increase intensity and volume
- Weekly structure: 2-3 quality runs + 1-2 easy runs
- Long run: Progressive buildup (20-35+ km)
- Intent: Peak fitness development

### Taper Phase (Weeks 13-16)
- Focus: Recovery and peak race readiness
- Weekly structure: Reduced volume, maintain intensity
- Long run: Final confidence run (20-25 km) → shorter finale
- Intent: Arrive fresh and strong at race start

## Progression Rules

1. **Conservative Load Increases** — Never increase weekly volume >10% week-to-week
2. **Respect Hard/Easy Balance** — Quality sessions require 48-72 hours between them
3. **Missed Sessions** — Adapt future weeks rather than compensate immediately
4. **Down Weeks** — Every 3-4 weeks, reduce volume by 30-40% for recovery

## Customization

These are **defaults**, not absolutes:
- Adjust based on athlete availability (fewer runs per week, adjusted distribution)
- Adapt intensity if athlete shows fatigue or injury signals
- Shift long-run day if athlete preference or schedule demands it
- Modify taper aggressiveness based on recent fitness trends

## Reference

Based on methodology from [Corrida e Performance](https://www.youtube.com/@CorridaePerformance) and modern marathon training frameworks.
